articles.expand: With the European Central Bank refusing to pre-commit to any rate path, the euro looks stuck in “paralysed policymaker” mode. What does this mean for the euro?

articles.title: US Dollar Index posts modest gains above 98.00 on hawkish Fed pause despite weaker US GDP
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 98.15 during the Asian trading hours on Friday. The DXY posts modest gains on a hawkish hold from the US Federal Reserve (Fed).

articles.title: ASEAN ministers warn Middle East war threatens energy security and regional growth
articles.expand: ASEAN economic ministers warn Middle East war threatens regional energy security and growth. Strait of Hormuz carries ~25% of global seaborne oil and LNG; over 80% destined for Asia. Freight, insurance and logistics costs rising sharply. Summary: The ASEAN Economic Community Council issued a joint statement on Friday warning that the Middle East wa...

articles.title: Japanese Yen weakens as softer Tokyo CPI and Iran tensions counter intervention warnings
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair is seen building on the previous day's late rebound from the vicinity of mid-155.00s, or over a two-month low, and gaining some positive traction during the Asian session on Friday.

articles.title: May meeting, RBA set for third straight hike as Hormuz closure drives inflation surge
articles.expand: Reuters poll: RBA to hike 25bps to 4.35% on May 5, say 30 of 33 economists. Over a third now see rates at 4.60%+ by year-end vs none in March. Strait of Hormuz closure keeps oil above $100; CPI at 4.1%. Summary: A Reuters poll of 33 economists conducted April 27 to 30 found 30 expect the RBA to raise its cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.35% at its...

articles.title: Japan manufacturing PMI hits 4-year high but Middle East stockpiling masks fragile demand
articles.expand: Japan April manufacturing PMI 55.1 vs 51.6 in March, best since January 2022. Output fastest since Feb 2014 but driven by stockpiling. Supply delays worst in 15 years. Input costs at 3.5-year high. Business confidence near 5-year low. Summary: The S P Global Japan Manufacturing PMI rose to 55.1 in April from 51.6 in March, its strongest reading sin...

articles.title: Japan head intervention official won't comment on FX or oil futures intervention
articles.expand: Japan's senior currency official Atsushi Mimura refused to confirm or deny reports of yen intervention after the currency breached 160 per dollar, as the BoJ's cautious stance, $120 oil and thin Golden Week liquidity keep the pressure firmly on. Summary: Atsushi Mimura, the Ministry of Finance's top official responsible for international finance an...

articles.title: Tokyo CPI misses forecast sharply, giving BoJ room to hold despite June hike signals
articles.expand: This is a yen-negative, JGB-positive print in the near term. The magnitude of the miss on core-core, coming in at 1.9% against a 2.3% expectation and prior, is significant enough to give the BoJ genuine cover to delay a June hike despite the hawkish signals delivered at the April meeting. Rate markets will likely push out their June hike pricing on...

articles.title: EUR/USD gathers strength above 1.1700 as ECB keeps rates steady
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair attracts some buyers to around 1.1730 during the early Asian session on Friday. The Euro (EUR) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) after the European Central Bank (ECB) kept interest rates on hold at its April meeting.

articles.title: Japan: Tokyo area April CPI headline 1.5% y/y (expected 1.7%, prior 1.4%)

articles.title: USD Slides As U.S. Core PCE and Advance GDP Point to Stagflationary Pressures Building
articles.expand: The U.S. advance Q1 2026 GDP missed expectations at 2.0% while core PCE inflation surged to 3.2% annually, a combination that supported the case for the Fed to stay on hold.

articles.title: NZ consumer confidence hits three-year low as Middle East oil shock bites
